title: Window Functions, Analytical Grain, and Time-Aware SQL · Data Contract Brief: Window Partition Order
summary: Service leaders need rankings, running effort, previous-event comparisons, and rolling metrics without collapsing detail rows. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql window partition order increment in a time-aware service-level analytics report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define window partition, order, and frame independently from final result ordering.
Distinguish rollback, crash recovery, backup, replication, and tested point-in-time restoration.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Compare each ticket with its team's effort and severity peers, retain teams with no resolved work, and rank ties deliberately. Build named query stages, one changed window of 3 rows, and a reconciliation total. Competency probe: Define window partition, order, and frame independently from final result ordering. A defensible solution applies Window Partition Order, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a time-aware service-level analytics report.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-window-partition-order — Define window partition, order, and frame independently from final result ordering.
SELECT team_id, ticket_id, severity,
       ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY team_id ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id) AS arrival,
       LAG(opened_at) OVER (PARTITION BY team_id ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id) AS previous_opened,
       COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY team_id) AS team_ticket_count
FROM tickets
ORDER BY team_id, arrival;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Window Partition Order evidence · Theory Window Partition Order
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-window-partition-order-1hpc39" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; window partition, ordering, frame, rank, or time evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Window Partition Order evidence · Theory Window Partition Order.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Window Partition Order into observable performance evidence. For Build Window Partition Order evidence · Theory Window Partition Order, keep Window Partition Order connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-window-partition-order-1hpc39
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT ticket_id, severity,
       DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY CASE severity WHEN 'critical' THEN 1 WHEN 'high' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END) AS severity_rank
FROM tickets ORDER BY severity_rank, ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
